# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 142

This is turn 142. Turns 1 through 141 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 142.

## Turn 142 — the three-lemma source interface

The frozen \(h_0\) candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
g0 = sigma2^2
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0
dg = d0^g0
hcomm = dg^-1*d0^-1*dg*d0
h0 = (x0^g0)*x0*dg*d0*d0^2*hcomm

h0*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. In the mathematical presentation, every exponent \(40491355905\) denotes the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); that integer is only its required residue modulo \(85667662080\). 

Turns 138–141 reduce all source dependence in the four recurrence branches to three precise propositions. Packaging them reveals that they form a closed interface: no higher word coefficient can enter the recurrence except through one of these propositions. 

### Proposition I — marked boundary

Let \(W_h=\langle\!\langle x_0,x_1\rangle\!\rangle\). Then

\[
\Gamma_h/W_h\simeq
T=\langle \sigma,\tau\mid \tau^\sigma=\tau^2\rangle
\]

and

\[
\Gamma_h(2)\simeq
\Pi_*=
\left\langle \sigma,x_0,x_1\ \middle|\
x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]=1
\right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}.
\]

The first assertion follows by putting \(x_0=x_1=1\); all \(\omega _2\)-projections of odd tame inertia vanish. For the second, every pro-\(2\) quotient has

\[
\tau=1,\qquad \sigma_2=\sigma,\qquad
u_i=x_i,\qquad d_0=c_0=1,
\]

and therefore

\[
h_0=x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0,\qquad
u_1^{-1}x_1^\sigma=[x_1,\sigma].
\]

The local maximal pro-\(2\) group has the same **marked** presentation. Starting from

\[
\langle a,b,c\mid a^2b^4[b,c]=1\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}
\]

and setting

\[
\sigma=b,\qquad x_0=(ab^2)^{-1},\qquad x_1=c
\]

gives the displayed relation while preserving

\[
\nu_{\mathrm{ur}}(\sigma)=1,\qquad
\nu_{\mathrm{ur}}(x_0)=\nu_{\mathrm{ur}}(x_1)=0.
\]

Thus candidate and local source have the identical generator-marked boundary, not merely abstractly isomorphic Demuškin quotients.

### Proposition II — natural two-jet duality

Fix a finite lower quotient \(C\), a lower tuple \(\rho\), and an elementary \(\mathbf F_2[C]\)-module \(A\). The candidate lifting problem has deformation complex

\[
\mathcal C_\rho(A):
0\longrightarrow A
\longrightarrow A^4
\longrightarrow A^2
\longrightarrow0.
\]

Finite-Heisenberg evaluation gives a natural chain map

\[
D_{\rho,A}:\mathcal C_\rho(A)
\longrightarrow
\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathbf F_2}
\bigl(\mathcal C_\rho(A^\vee),\mathbf F_2\bigr)[-2].
\]

Naturality means that every \(C\)-module map \(f:A\to A'\) satisfies the strict adjoint identity for the mixed central term. Consequently, for every short exact sequence

\[
0\to A'\to A\to A''\to0,
\]

there is a short exact sequence of mapping cones. Quasi-isomorphism on simple modules therefore extends by dévissage to every possibly nonsemisimple elementary module.

For the repaired word, the exact class-two identity

\[
h_0=\phi(X)X D^{-1}\phi(D)
\]

shows that \(h_0\) evaluates exactly as \(X^2\) in both projector branches. Hence its simple-module pairings are

\[
\langle c,\lambda\rangle_h=
\begin{cases}
\lambda(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
\lambda\!\left((1+U+U^{-1})c\right),&V^T=0,
\end{cases}
\qquad U=S^{\omega _2}.
\]

The second operator is invertible because \(U\) has \(2\)-power order and

\[
1+U+U^{-1}=U^{-1}(U^2+U+1).
\]

The scalar initial form is still

\[
B^2+AC+CA.
\]

Therefore, for every elementary active module \(A\),

\[
\operatorname{Ob}_\rho(A)^\vee\simeq (A^\vee)^C,
\qquad
\dim Z_\rho^1(A)=2\dim A+\dim(A^\vee)^C.
\]

This proposition supplies exactly the elementary-lift multiplicities, Fourier completeness, and nonzero-edge half-fibre used by the first three nonterminal recurrence branches.

### Proposition III — natural determinant pushouts

At a rank-one zero-edge node, every target-derived quadratic pushout descends from the active module \(M\) to a nonzero nonsingular invariant form

\[
q:V=M/T_0\longrightarrow\mathbf F_2
\]

on the nontrivial simple head.

All such forms lie in one \(\operatorname{Aut}_H(V)\)-orbit. Indeed, put \(E=\operatorname{End}_H(V)\). If \(b,b'\) are the polar forms of \(q,q'\), then

\[
b'(v,w)=b(av,w)
\]

for a unique self-adjoint \(a\in E^\times\). The norm map from \(E^\times\) to the fixed field of the adjoint involution is surjective, so \(a=u^*u\). The forms \(q(uv)\) and \(q'(v)\) then have the same polar form; their difference is an \(H\)-invariant linear form and hence vanishes.

Both obstruction constructions are natural under this isometry:

\[
Q_{h,q}(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
q(c)+b(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0,
\end{cases}
\]

while \(Q_{\mathrm{loc},q}\) is the connecting quadratic form of the corresponding extraspecial extension.

For every invariant \(q\),

\[
\sum_{c\in H_\rho^1(V)}
(-1)^{Q_{\Gamma,q}(c)}
=
\epsilon(V)\,2^{\dim(V)/2},
\]

for both \(\Gamma=\Gamma_h\) and \(\Gamma=G_{\mathbf Q_2}\), where

\[
\epsilon(V)=
\begin{cases}
-1,&V\ \text{unramified},\\
+1,&V\ \text{ramified}.
\end{cases}
\]

Equivalently,

\[
\#Q_{\Gamma,q}^{-1}(0)
=
2^{d-1}+\epsilon(V)2^{d/2-1}.
\]

Radical Fourier pushouts do not alter \(q\); the marking-preserving shear restricts to the identity on \(V\). These operations only translate the \(H^1(V)\)-variable and replace an inflated base class by another strictly smaller target-derived class.

### Closed-interface theorem

The source-sensitive inputs of the four recurrences are now exactly:

| Recurrence branch | Required source proposition |
|---|---|
| Terminal scalar branch | Proposition I |
| \(R=1\) elementary branch | Proposition II |
| \(|R|\ge4\) Fourier branch | Proposition II |
| \(R=C_2\), nonzero edge | Proposition II |
| \(R=C_2\), zero edge | Propositions II and III |

Every subgroup incidence, central pushout, exact-image subtraction, active-block choice, and recursive child is finite target data. Every recursive child has strictly smaller marked-kernel order. Hence any two marked sources satisfying Propositions I–III obey the same four triangular recurrences and have identical terminal values; strong induction gives identical framed finite counts.

This packages the source-replacement argument without appealing to agreement of fifth or higher Zassenhaus coefficients. Those coefficients are either already contained in the complete boundary \(\Pi_*\), or their effect is transmitted through a strictly smaller recurrence node.

**ESTABLISHED**: Packaged the corrected marked boundary, natural finite-Heisenberg deformation duality, and natural determinant-pushout theorem as a closed three-proposition source interface. Proved branch by branch that these are exactly the source inputs consumed by the four rank-halving recurrences; no additional higher-order source invariant has an independent recurrence slot.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The target-side derivation of recurrences (128.1)–(128.4), including every subgroup-incidence multiplicity and the factor-eight central-cover transform, still needs an independent line-by-line audit.

**NEXT**: Re-derive the four recurrences solely from exact-image stratification, Fourier zero-fibre inversion, and the central-double-cover lift transform, keeping embeddings rather than subgroup isomorphism types. This is the remaining uniform audit before verifier submission.

[CONTINUE]
